name: Azure Service Bus (Inbound)
description: Azure Service Bus inbound endpoint acts as a message consumer for Azure Service Bus. It receives messages from a configured queue or topic subscription and injects them into the mediation flow.

operations.name: init
operations.description: Initialize Azure Service Bus Inbound Endpoint

params.name: name
params.description: Unique identifier for the ASB event integration.

params.name: connectionString
params.description: Azure Service Bus connection string.

params.name: entityType
params.description: Type of Service Bus entity to consume from.

params.name: queueName
params.description: Name of the Service Bus queue.

params.name: topicName
params.description: Name of the Service Bus topic.

params.name: subscriptionName
params.description: Name of the topic subscription.

params.name: sessionEnabled
params.description: Set to true if the target queue or topic subscription is session-enabled.

params.name: contentType
params.description: Content type used for message parsing. Takes priority over the message's content type. If neither is set, the SOAP builder is used.

params.name: coordination
params.description: In a clustered setup, this will run the inbound only in a single worker node.

params.name: inboundVariableName
params.description: Name of the variable that holds the structured message data (attributes and headers). Accessible in the mediation flow via this variable name.

params.name: receiveMode
params.description: Message receive mode. PEEK_LOCK allows settling messages after processing. RECEIVE_AND_DELETE removes messages immediately.

params.name: maxConcurrentConsumers
params.description: Maximum number of concurrent consumers processing messages.

params.name: maxConcurrentSessions
params.description: Maximum number of sessions to process concurrently.

params.name: maxConcurrentConsumersPerSession
params.description: Maximum number of concurrent consumers processing messages within a single session. Messages within a session are processed in order when set to 1.

params.name: sessionIdleTimeoutMs
params.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a message on an idle session before releasing the session lock and acquiring the next available session. If not set, defaults to the Try Timeout value.

params.name: prefetchCount
params.description: Number of messages to fetch from the broker and buffer locally for faster processing. Higher values improve throughput but use more memory. 0 disables prefetching.

params.name: maxLockDurationMs
params.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds to keep the message lock alive during processing. The lock is automatically renewed in the background until this duration is reached (PEEK_LOCK mode only).

params.name: messageProcessingTimeoutMs
params.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the message to be fully processed before timing out. Must be lower than Max Lock Duration. If the timeout is exceeded, the message lock will expire and the message will be redelivered.

params.name: maxRetries
params.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for transient connection or network failures.

params.name: retryDelayMs
params.description: Initial delay in milliseconds between retry attempts. Increases exponentially with each retry up to Retry Max Delay.

params.name: retryMaxDelayMs
params.description: Maximum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts.

params.name: tryTimeoutMs
params.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds to wait for a single operation (e.g., receive, settle) to complete before timing out.
